title: BAA Compliance Monitoring
spl:
index=cloud sourcetype="casb:activity" tag=phi
| lookup baa_vendors vendor OUTPUT baa_signed subprocessor_ok
| where isnull(baa_signed) OR baa_signed="false"
| stats count by vendor, app, user
| sort -count
description: Ensures business associates' systems sending PHI are authorized and logged per contract.
value: Ensures business associates' systems sending PHI are authorized and logged per contract.
implementation: Maintain vendor inventory with BAA dates; block or proxy unsanctioned apps with PHI.
visualization: Table (non-BAA usage), Bar chart (vendors), Status (BAA coverage %).
knownFalsePositives: BAA monitoring noise when SaaS metadata lags, vendors rotate IPs, or new connectors come online; refresh vendor inventory in the workflow.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Cloud app security, firewall.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="casb:activity"`, `sourcetype="pan:traffic"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Maintain vendor inventory with BAA dates; block or proxy unsanctioned apps with PHI.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cloud sourcetype="casb:activity" tag=phi
| lookup baa_vendors vendor OUTPUT baa_signed subprocessor_ok
| where isnull(baa_signed) OR baa_signed="false"
| stats count by vendor, app, user
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**BAA Compliance Monitoring** — Ensures business associates' systems sending PHI are authorized and logged per contract.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="casb:activity"`, `sourcetype="pan:traffic"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Cloud app security, firewall. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cloud; **sourcetype**: casb:activity. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cloud, sourcetype="casb:activity", tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(baa_signed) OR baa_signed="false"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by vendor, app, user** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (non-BAA usage), Bar chart (vendors), Status (BAA coverage %).
grandmaExplanation: In Healthcare, we pay close attention to "BAA Compliance Monitoring" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
